metadata.headline: Task reactivity in this ABPM cohort is primarily a physical-stage SBP and HR phenomenon, with the clearest participant-level signals coming from lower nocturnal dipping, smaller battery-2 Stroop interference, and a directional increase with BP load.

findings.claim: Physical-task reactivity exceeds cognitive-task reactivity within participants, driven by SBP and HR rather than DBP.
findings.evidence: Paired within-participant comparison of mean cognitive versus physical task ratios by channel.

validation.rederived: Matched using subgroup-level physical-minus-cognitive gaps by diagnostic status.
validation.simpsons_result: No reversal: SBP and HR gaps stay positive in healthy and non-healthy subgroups; DBP stays approximately flat.
bias_checks: selection: small single-cohort sample limits generalization
composition shift: checked by healthy-status subgroups, no sign reversal
metric drift: same ratio definitions across participants

findings.claim: The physical-task surplus is concentrated in stage 2, where SBP rises further and HR remains elevated while cognitive SBP softens in stage 2.
findings.evidence: Stage-specific paired comparison of stage 1 vs stage 2 ratios for cognitive and physical tasks.

validation.rederived: Confirmed from independent stage-specific contrasts and a separate battery-2 DBP correlation.
validation.simpsons_result: No reversal after splitting by health status; stage-2 physical SBP remains above stage-1 in both strata.
bias_checks: selection: stage-2 measurements missing for some participants
composition shift: similar direction in healthy and non-healthy strata
base rate: ratios normalize stage comparisons within participant

findings.claim: Lower nocturnal SBP dipping is associated with stronger physical-task reactivity.
findings.evidence: Spearman correlation between `sbp_dip_%` and `physical_reactivity_mean` plus tertile drill-down.

validation.rederived: Matched using an independent subgroup-by-diagnosis re-derivation rather than the tertile summary.
validation.simpsons_result: Direction remains stable by diagnostic stratum; no aggregate reversal detected.
bias_checks: selection: small n inflates uncertainty
composition shift: checked by diagnostic strata
lookahead: all predictors are contemporaneous participant-level features

findings.claim: Higher ambulatory blood-pressure load aligns with stronger physical-task reactivity.
findings.evidence: Spearman correlation between `bp_load_%` and `physical_reactivity_mean` plus tertile drill-down.

validation.rederived: Matched using an independent subgroup-by-diagnosis re-derivation rather than the tertile summary.
validation.simpsons_result: Direction remains stable by diagnostic stratum; no aggregate reversal detected.
bias_checks: selection: small n inflates uncertainty
composition shift: checked by diagnostic strata
lookahead: all predictors are contemporaneous participant-level features

findings.claim: Smaller second-battery Stroop interference aligns with stronger physical-task reactivity, especially in physical-stage DBP.
findings.evidence: Spearman correlation between `stroop_effect_battery_2` and `physical_reactivity_mean` plus tertile drill-down.

validation.rederived: Matched using an independent subgroup-by-diagnosis re-derivation rather than the tertile summary.
validation.simpsons_result: Direction remains stable by diagnostic stratum; no aggregate reversal detected.
bias_checks: selection: small n inflates uncertainty
composition shift: checked by diagnostic strata
lookahead: all predictors are contemporaneous participant-level features

findings.claim: Healthy versus non-healthy classification separates baseline blood-pressure load far more strongly than task-reactivity magnitude.
findings.evidence: Mann-Whitney comparison of `bp_load_%` by healthy vs non-healthy monitoring diagnosis.

validation.rederived: Confirmed with an independent non-parametric group contrast and a same-strata check on physical reactivity.
validation.simpsons_result: No paradox because the primary comparison is already stratified by diagnosis.
bias_checks: selection: imbalance between healthy and non-healthy groups
base rate: effect expressed against group means and p-value
composition shift: task reactivity remains similar despite baseline-load separation

findings.claim: Healthy participants show markedly smaller battery-2 Stroop interference, while mean task reactivity remains similar across diagnostic groups.
findings.evidence: Mann-Whitney comparison of `stroop_effect_battery_2` by healthy vs non-healthy monitoring diagnosis.

validation.rederived: Confirmed with an independent non-parametric group contrast and a same-strata check on physical reactivity.
validation.simpsons_result: No paradox because the primary comparison is already stratified by diagnosis.
bias_checks: selection: imbalance between healthy and non-healthy groups
base rate: effect expressed against group means and p-value
composition shift: task reactivity remains similar despite baseline-load separation

recommendations.action: Prioritize physical-task SBP and HR endpoints, especially stage 2, as the primary physiological readouts in the next mechanistic study.
recommendations.success_metric: Replicate the physical-vs-cognitive SBP and HR gap in an expanded cohort with pre-registered thresholds.

recommendations.action: Stratify future analyses by nocturnal SBP dipping and ABPM load before aggregating task responses.
recommendations.success_metric: Prospective models retain the same direction and improve explained variance over unstratified models.

recommendations.action: Test whether battery-2 Stroop interference is a surrogate of preserved autonomic reserve rather than a marker of dysregulation.
recommendations.success_metric: A replication dataset confirms the inverse Stroop-effect to physical-reactivity relationship after multivariable adjustment.

beats.title: Physical tasks produce the main hemodynamic excursion in this cohort
beats.purpose: Establish the dominant modality before looking for drivers.

beats.title: The modality gap is a stage-2 SBP and HR effect, not a uniform rise across all channels
beats.purpose: Show that the aggregate modality gap is not homogeneous.

beats.title: Higher BP load and lower nocturnal dipping concentrate the strongest physical responders
beats.purpose: Drill from overall modality into physiologic recovery and load strata.

beats.title: Participants with smaller battery-2 Stroop interference show stronger physical-task reactivity
beats.purpose: Connect the hemodynamic pattern to cognitive performance rather than treating it as isolated physiology.

beats.title: Diagnostic status separates baseline load and battery-2 performance more than task-reactivity magnitude
beats.purpose: Keep the health-status secondary question from being overstated as a reactivity driver.

beats.title: Recommendations
beats.purpose: Translate the validated findings into next-study priorities.
